text:
Thank you Brother Bosworth. Good evening friends. Very happy to be here this evening to minister in the Name of our Lord Jesus to the sick and afflicted, needy people. And I trust, by God's grace that tonight that He will give to us the greatest night we've had in all the services, because of His presence being with us.
And now before we go much farther, I… sometimes I forget these handkerchiefs. I used to mention it in the meetings, but I… about sending to my home for handkerchiefs, and I always send out hundreds of them a week. But it's a… reason I don't say that, I happened to think that so many people on the radio are trying to say, "Just write us a letter." They're only trying to get your address, so they could make you kind of a mailing list, but I--I don't want to do that. I just… anything I could help you to, I'd be glad to do it, but… And if you don't have your handkerchief here; I'll pray for it right here if you got it, and if you don't have it, why you just send in. It don't cost you nothing, we'd be glad to send it, help you to everything we can. Send literature with it, to instruct you on how to do.
You know at the same time that your… these handkerchiefs goes on you… all the way around the world, there's prayer being made at the same time. Ev… At three o'clock… nine o'clock at morning, twelve o'clock at noon, and three o'clock in the afternoon Eastern Standard, I mean Central Standard Time, completely around the world, there's a circle, at that same time from my own meetings; results of them, hook up around the world. And some of them get up at twelve o'clock at night, two o'clock in the morning to offer prayer. Now I have more testimonies over the handkerchiefs, than I have testimonies out among meetings. That's right. Cause, it reaches more. Send out hundreds and hundreds of them a week. So now, let's just bow our heads a moment, and ask God to bless these people.
Our Heavenly Father, it's with great delight that we bring these people before Thee. Laying here tonight is these handkerchiefs that represent sick and afflicted people. We love them, because they love You. And Thou has brought us nigh unto Thee by the washing of Thy blood of the water of the Word, and has brought these people to relationship with us. And now we are sons and daughters of God, brothers and sisters in Christ. And our hearts go out for each other in sickness and in trouble, when one member suffers all the members suffer from the effects. Father, in my heart and in our hearts tonight, we suffer with the burden that You'll heal all these people. We want to see them well. We want to see them up, going about, and well and healthy.
And then in the Bible, we read that where they taken from the body of Saint Paul, handkerchiefs and aprons, and the sickness was healed and unclean spirits went out. And now I have the Bible laying on these handkerchiefs, and Father, we pray that You'll bless each one of them, and may they be healed, each and every one. For we ask it in Jesus Name, Thy Son, Amen. May the Lord bless each one of these handkerchiefs for its intended purpose.
Now in the Word just for a few moments, I would like to read, and I promise you tonight that I would tell you about the vision that's going to take place pretty soon. And I--I trust that God will--will give to each one of you the… His blessing, while we're reading the Word and trying to explain it. And some of you want to put down the vision, of course, of what's going to take place, you're more than welcome. Anything that's ever been said concerning when it's THUS SAITH THE LORD tacked onto it, why you'll find out, I'll lay my life right to it. It'll be just that way.
And here not long ago, two years before it come to pass, told of a little boy being raised in Finland, you read the story of it in the book. And by the way, now we're… as I say, we're not book selling, because we don't… we just let you have the book. But if you want one for a Chirstmas present, or some of your friends, tonight's your last chance because we don't sell on the Sabbath. And any of the pictures, take one of them home with you, we'd be glad for you to have it; just at the cost, just at What it cost us to get it, get it here to you, you're welcome to it. Ane we don't commercialize on anything at all, not a thing.
And--and our meetings, if God don't supply our needs, then it's time for me to get--get off the field and go home, see. But when He supplies my needs, why I just keep on staying where ever He leads me to go. And if He has no place to led me, why then I just go where ever the managers picks out a place to go. And I love people, and I love to minister to them, and I love them because they love Christ. And if they love Christ, then they bound to love me. You can't love--you can't love Christ and hate me. You--you got to love me too, because I'm--I'm His servant. And I have to love you, because you're His servant; and I happily and most gracefully, in my heart, do love you, everyone. That's true.
Now I want to read something from His Word, which found in Acts the 17th chapter. And this was included into the vision, so it was of a great man, Paul. It's the 21st verse of the 27th chapter of Acts.
After a long abstinence Paul stood forth in the midst of them, and said, Sirs, you should have hearkened unto me, and not have loosed from Crete, and to have caused this harm and loss.
But now I exhort you to be of good cheer: for there stood by me… (Beg your pardon? The 22nd verse)
And… I exhort you to be of a good cheer: for there shall be no loss of any man's life among you, but of the ship.
For there stood by me this night an angel of God, whose I am, and whose servant I am.
Saying, Fear not, Paul; thou must be brought before Caesar: and, lo, God has given unto thee all them that sail with thee.
Wherefore, sirs, be of good cheer: for I believe God, that it shall be even as it was told me.
May the Lord add His blessing to His Word.
text_no:
Takk, Bror Bosworth. God kveld, venner. Det er en stor glede å være her i kveld for å betjene de syke og trengende i vår Herre Jesu Navn. Og jeg håper, ved Guds nåde, at denne kvelden blir den største vi har hatt i alle møtene, på grunn av Hans nærvær blant oss.
Før vi går videre, vil jeg nevne noe angående lommetørklene. Tidligere nevnte jeg det i møtene, men jeg har sluttet å be folk sende til mitt hjem for lommetørklær. Jeg sender ut hundrevis av dem hver uke. Grunnen til at jeg ikke nevner det lenger, er at mange på radioen sier "bare skriv til oss." De prøver kun å få adressen din for å lage en adresseringsliste. Jeg ønsker ikke å gjøre det. Jeg vil bare hjelpe deg så mye jeg kan. Hvis du ikke har med deg lommetørkleet ditt, kan du sende det inn, uten kostnad. Vi sender gjerne med noe litteratur som forklarer hva du skal gjøre.
Du vet, samtidig som disse lommetørklene sendes ut til deg, blir det bedt hele veien rundt om i verden. Ved tre ulike tider - klokken ni om morgenen, tolv på dagen og tre på ettermiddagen, Central Standard Time - blir det bedt kontinuerlig. Og vi har flere vitnesbyrd over lommetørklene enn fra møtene, fordi de når flere. Send gjerne inn dine lommetørklær så skal vi be for dem.
La oss bøye våre hoder et øyeblikk og be Gud velsigne disse menneskene. 
Vår Himmelske Far, vi legger disse menneskene i Dine hender. Her ligger lommetørklene som representerer syke og plaget mennesker. Vi elsker dem fordi de elsker Deg. Du har brakt oss nær ved Ditt blods vask og ved Vanndåpen og Ordet, og har brakt disse menneskene til et fellesskap med oss. Nå er vi sønner og døtre av Gud, brødre og søstre i Kristus. Vi lider med hverandre i sykdom og trøbbel; når ett medlem lider, lider alle. Far, vi bærer i våre hjerter en byrde for å se alle disse menneskene friske. Vi ønsker å se dem oppreisst, sunne og friske.
Bibelen forteller oss at fra Paulus' kropp ble det tatt lommetørklær og forkler, og sykdom ble helbredet og urene ånder forlot. Nå ligger Bibelen på disse lommetørklene, og vi ber om at Du velsigner dem og helbreder hver og en. Vi ber i Jesu Navn, Din Sønn, Amen. Må Herren velsigne hvert av disse lommetørklene for deres tiltenkte hensikt.
Nå vil jeg lese fra Ordet et øyeblikk. Jeg lovet å fortelle om visjonen som snart skal skje. Jeg håper Gud vil velsigne dere mens vi leser Ordet og forklarer det. Hvis noen vil notere visjonen, er dere velkomne til det. Alt som er merket med "Slik sier Herren", står jeg ved.
For ikke så lenge siden, to år før det skjedde, ble det profetert om en liten gutt som skulle bli oppreist i Finland. Dere har lest historien i boken. Vi selger ikke bøker, men hvis dere vil ha en til julegave eller til venner, er i kveld siste sjanse, for vi selger ikke på sabbaten. Dere kan ta med et av bildene hjem, til kostpris. Vi kommersialiserer ikke noe.
Om Gud ikke dekker våre behov, er det på tide for meg å dra hjem. Men når Han dekker dem, fortsetter jeg dit Han leder meg. Hvis Han ikke har noen steder å lede meg, går jeg dit manageren foreslår. Jeg elsker mennesker, og jeg elsker å tjene dem, fordi de elsker Kristus. Og hvis de elsker Kristus, må de elske meg. Du kan ikke elske Kristus og hate meg. Du må også elske meg, fordi jeg er Hans tjener, og jeg elsker dere alle.
Nå vil jeg lese noe fra Hans Ord. Vi finner det i Apostlenes gjerninger, kapittel 27, vers 21-25:
"Etter en lang tid uten mat, sto Paulus fram blant dem og sa: 'Sirs, dere skulle ha lyttet til meg og ikke seilt fra Kreta og forårsaket denne skaden og tapet.
Men nå oppmuntrer jeg dere til å være ved godt mot. For denne natten sto det ved meg en engel fra Gud, hvis jeg tilhører, og som jeg tjener.
Han sa: Frykt ikke, Paulus; du må føres fram for Cæsar. Og se, Gud har gitt deg alle dem som seiler med deg.
Derfor, sirs, vær ved godt mot; for jeg tror Gud at det skal bli slik som det ble sagt meg.'"
Må Herren legge Sin velsignelse til Sitt Ord.

text: Now, could you imagine, on a stormy sea, and all hopes, and knowing they were out of the will of God, 'cause God had warned Paul not to loose from Crete, but he couldn't help it. But, they went ahead. The shipmaster, he knowed more about it than what the Holy Spirit did, you know. So, he just loosed and went ahead anyhow. And they got out on the ship there in the sea, and there come up a great storm, and for several days and nights there was no moon, no stars, fourteen days and nights. And all hopes that they'd ever be saved was all give up and gone. They was bound to be drowned. They throwed out the tackle of the ship and everything, and still just let it drive, and it was just going anyway. And Paul went down into the galley and prayed. There was an Angel that stood by Paul, and said … told him the message.
text_no: Kan du forestille deg å være på et opprørt hav, uten håp og vel vitende om at de var utenfor Guds vilje? Gud hadde advart Paulus mot å dra fra Kreta, men han kunne ikke stoppe det. Skipets kaptein mente han visste bedre enn Den Hellige Ånd, så de seilte videre likevel. Ute på sjøen kom det en kraftig storm, og i fjorten dager og netter var det verken måne eller stjerner å se. Alt håp om å bli reddet var ute, og de var sikre på at de kom til å drukne. De kastet skipets tackle over bord og lot det drive fritt. Paulus gikk ned i byssa og ba. En engel kom til Paulus og ga ham et budskap.

text:
And I can just see that little Jew come up on top of the ship where all these men were weeping and crying. They will never see their wives again. Never see their children again. Just like the doctor tells you that "You just get ready to die. The cancer's going to kill you. And you will never get up from the bed no more, and your days are finished." And they're weeping. They'll never be up again or something like that. But this little Jew went down in there and prayed through until he touched God.
And in a vision, standing by his side, he saw an Angel which brought him the message, the Angel of the Lord. And he come up and he said, "Wherefore, sirs, be of a good cheer: for the angel of God, whose servant I am, stood by me last night and said, 'Fear not, Paul; for thou must be brought before Caesar: and, lo, God has give all them that sail with you to you. There won't be any loss of any man.' So he said, wherefore, sirs, be of a good cheer: for I believe God, that it shall be just as it was shown me."
Now, I'm so thankful. Wouldn't you like to have been there when that was taking place on that ship? Wouldn't we had a wonderful time? Just to rejoice and shout with Paul, and walk up and down the galleys of that ship. Why, I couldn't have kept still. Could you? I just wonder why we keep still, and the same thing taking place here night after night. See? That's what I wonder. The same thing. If we would have been there, we'd have been just like we are now. The great things of God is so great, and yet people try to imagine so much out in here somewhere in the supernatural, a hocus-pocus like, when it's right down here.
text_no:
Jeg ser for meg den lille jøden som kommer opp på dekk hvor alle mennene gråter og sørger. De vil aldri se sine koner igjen. Aldri se sine barn igjen. Akkurat som når legen sier til deg: "Gjør deg klar til å dø. Kreften vil ta livet ditt, og du vil aldri komme deg opp av sengen igjen. Dine dager er talte." De gråter fordi de aldri vil komme seg opp igjen. Men denne lille jøden gikk ned og ba til han rørte ved Gud.
I en visjon som stod ved hans side, så han en Engel som brakte ham budskapet, Herrens Engel. Han kom opp og sa: "Vær ved godt mot, mine herrer, for i natt stod en Guds Engel, hvis tjener jeg er, ved min side og sa: 'Frykt ikke, Paulus; for du må komme frem for keiseren, og se, Gud har gitt alle som seiler med deg til deg. Ingen skal miste livet.' Så han sa, vær ved godt mot, mine herrer, for jeg tror Gud, at det skal bli slik som det ble vist meg."
Jeg er så takknemlig. Ville du ikke ha ønsket å være der da dette skjedde på skipet? Hadde vi ikke hatt en vidunderlig tid? Bare for å glede oss og rope med Paulus, og gå opp og ned i gangene på skipet. Jeg kunne ikke ha holdt meg rolig. Kunne du? Jeg lurer på hvorfor vi holder oss rolig, når det samme skjer her kveld etter kveld. Ser du? Det er det jeg lurer på. Det samme skjer. Hvis vi hadde vært der, ville vi ha vært akkurat slik vi er nå. Guds store gjerninger er så store, og likevel prøver folk å forestille seg så mye ut i det overnaturlige, en slags magi, når det skjer rett her.

text:
I can teach a five-year-old child the kingdom of God, better that I can teach a man that's got a degree out of college. I can teach him Christ better. He's simple. He just listens to what you say. Watch coming to the platform. You will never see God turn a child down. He don't do it. No, sir. Because they're just simple in faith. They believe it. And when they believe it, that just settles it. God says so, and that takes care of it. The little child will believe it, but, oh, my, we know better, you know. We know all the angles, and just how could it be. And we got to figure out, "Now, how could this be?" But it will be anyhow.
What caused me to go to Africa on this trip? Just to … brings up this vision. And I'm watching that clock there, but I'm way early, so I got plenty of time. It's only twenty-five minutes till four, so I suppose I have a whole lot of time. But some of you brothers let me know about, when it's just about time to start the prayer line, in about a half-hour.
text_no:
Jeg kan lære et fem år gammelt barn om Guds rike bedre enn jeg kan lære en mann med en universitetsgrad. Jeg kan lære barnet om Kristus enklere. Barnet er enkelt; det lytter bare til hva du sier. Se når barnet kommer til plattformen. Du vil aldri se Gud avvise et barn. Han gjør det ikke. Nei, fordi de har enkel tro. De tror på det, og det avgjør saken. Gud sier det, og det er nok. Barnet vil tro det, mens vi ofte tror vi vet bedre. Vi analyserer og prøver å finne ut, "Hvordan kan dette være?" Men det skjer uansett.
Hva fikk meg til å dra til Afrika på denne turen? Dette fører til en visjon. Jeg ser på klokken, men jeg er tidlig, så jeg har god tid. Klokken er bare tjuefem minutter på fire, så jeg har masse tid. Men noen av dere brødre, gi meg beskjed når det er tid for å starte bønnekøen, om omtrent en halvtime.

text:
I was with Brother Bosworth. It was the night the Angel of the Lord's picture was taken. How many has got that picture, now, in your possession? Thank you. I'm sure it will be a great blessing to you.
Here sometime ago, there was a lady at the hospital at the city I live. The picture was setting on the table. The doctor had told here she couldn't get well. This is her story, she said looking at … not at her brother, but to the Angel of the Lord.
Now, if you will notice, friends, that is a Pillar of Fire. George J. Lacy, you read what he said. Now, that's the best that we got in the United States on that kind of work, on research. And when he said that day, said, "If the old hypocrite (he meant the unbeliever) says that there never could be scientifically proved that there was a supernatural being," but said, "he can't say that, now, because here's scientific proof that there is a supernatural being." For you hear what he said, "The Light struck the lens." See? And It was there. He said, "The mechanical eye of this camera won't take psychology." Said, "It's not psychology." Said, "I've said it was psychology, Mister Branham, myself," but said, "mechanical eye of this camera won't take psychology. The Light struck the lens, and the Being was there."
text_no:
Jeg var med Bror Bosworth. Det var kvelden bildet av Herrens Engels ble tatt. Hvor mange har det bildet nå i deres eie? Takk. Jeg er sikker på at det vil være til stor velsignelse for dere.
For en tid tilbake var det en dame på sykehuset i byen hvor jeg bor. Bildet sto på bordet. Legen hadde fortalt henne at hun ikke kunne bli frisk. I sin historie sa hun at hun ikke så på sin bror, men på Herrens Engel.
Venner, hvis dere legger merke til det, er det en Ildstøtte. George J. Lacy har skrevet hva han sa. Han er den beste i USA på dette forskningsområdet. Den dagen sa han: "Tidligere har hyklere (han mente vantro) hevdet at det aldri kunne bevises vitenskapelig at det eksisterte en overnaturlig vesen. Men nå kan de ikke si det lenger, for her er vitenskapelig bevis for at det finnes et overnaturlig vesen." For dere hørte hva han sa, "Lyset traff linsen." Ser dere? Og Det var der. Han sa: "Det mekaniske øyet i dette kameraet kan ikke fange psykologi. Det er ikke psykologi." Han fortsatte: "Jeg selv har sagt at det var psykologi, Mister Branham, men det mekaniske øyet i dette kameraet kan ikke fange psykologi. Lyset traff linsen, og Vesenet var der."

text:
You heard what Mr. Dr. Stomewell… How many reads The Voice of Healing, and read that article in there of Dr. Stomewell on that? He said, "Absolutely." Now, he don't know nothing about this picture yet. He was an atheist, just been converted a few weeks. But he said that that magnetic power of a believer in contact with God was so vital, he said that even he believed over the Christ, that that halo that was painted was absolutely could've been seen." Because it's the power.
Now, just think. He took a machine. You know, the human being is not made to lie. And if you're telling a lie, they can put you before a lie detector, and your nerves will react. And then they took this same thing and converted it to a way that they could tell whether it was truth or not, and put it on a person praying, and I think it taken, I forget how many thousand kilowatts to send a radio message around the world, and when this woman begin to pray at her death, there went out enough power from her till it registered over as far as the needle would go. Enough power leaving that woman, actually supernatural power that caught on this machine, some kind of a magnetic power of her prayer that was powerful enough to send a radio message fifty-five times around the world. That's scientific.
text_no:
Dere hørte hva Dr. Stomewell sa… Hvor mange av dere leser The Voice of Healing og leste den artikkelen av Dr. Stomewell der? Han sa: "Absolutt." Nå, han vet ingenting om dette bildet ennå. Han var en ateist som bare ble omvendt for noen uker siden. Men han sa at den magnetiske kraften til en troende i kontakt med Gud var så viktig at han til og med trodde at den glorien som ble malt over Kristus, absolutt kunne ha blitt sett. Fordi det er kraft.
Nå, bare tenk. Han brukte en maskin. Dere vet, mennesket er ikke laget for å lyve. Og hvis du lyver, kan de sette deg foran en løgnedetektor, og nervene dine vil reagere. Og så tok de det samme apparatet og omgjorde det på en måte som kunne avgjøre om det var sannhet eller ikke, og satte det på en person som ba. Jeg tror det krevde, jeg husker ikke nøyaktig hvor mange tusen kilowatt for å sende en radiomelding rundt om i verden, og da denne kvinnen begynte å be rett før sin død, ble det registrert nok kraft fra henne til at nålen gikk så langt den kunne. Det var nok kraft fra denne kvinnen, faktisk overnaturlig kraft som denne maskinen fanget, en slags magnetisk kraft fra hennes bønn som var kraftig nok til å sende en radiomelding femtifem ganger rundt jorden. Det er vitenskapelig.

text:
Men working around in science has found God. That's right. He lives out there. He is in the scientific world. They just touched around on His little basic play things, that He plays with, or has out there for them. Now, they're coming up into a place, in a category, where they can find Him.
Now, on this picture, Brother Bosworth brought me the picture of Florence Nightingale, and I'm sorry, we have no more of those books. They're out of print at the time. We wasn't financially able to … was about ten thousand dollars--cost about a dollar a piece to print them. They put them back in print, and they won't print less then ten thousand. So, we couldn't do it. But, when they first went out, why then, we sold all them, and they were gone.
text_no:
Vitenskapsmenn har funnet Gud. Det stemmer. Han er til stede i vitenskapens verden. De har bare så vidt berørt Hans grunnleggende leketing, de Han leker med eller har satt ut der til dem. Nå kommer de inn på et område hvor de kan finne Ham.
Når det gjelder dette bildet, tok Bror Bosworth med et bilde av Florence Nightingale til meg. Jeg beklager, men vi har ikke flere av de bøkene; de er utsolgt for øyeblikket. Vi hadde ikke økonomi til å … det ville kostet rundt ti tusen dollar—cirka en dollar stykket å trykke dem. De ville trykke dem opp igjen, men de trykker ikke mindre enn ti tusen eksemplarer. Så vi kunne ikke gjøre det. Men, da de først kom ut, solgte vi alle, og de ble utsolgt.

text:
Then, in there, he got a copyright from permitting the copyright to place this picture in there. The picture belongs to the Douglas Studios, not me. And then, in this picture, as I was saying a few moments ago, a woman in the hospital was looking at it, and was praying after her doctor told her she couldn't get well. She claimed that she seen that picture, that pillar of fire, come out of there and stand over where she was. I know the one thing, I don't know what taken place there, but the next day, she was sent home well. I don't know … I know that. And she was sent home well.
Now, it's His Being. If we will read the Scripture, we find out that it was a Pillar of Fire that led the children of Israel. That same Pillar of Fire, I believe, was over Jesus Christ. And I believe that He's risen from the dead, showing the same signs that He showed when he was here on earth. And it's that same Pillar of Fire that's over the church right now. The same Angel of God, that same presence.
text_no:
Der fikk han en opphavsrettstillatelse for å plassere bildet der. Bildet tilhører Douglas Studios, ikke meg. Som jeg nevnte for noen øyeblikk siden, var en kvinne på sykehuset og ba etter at legen hadde fortalt henne at hun ikke kunne bli frisk. Hun hevdet at hun så bildet, denne ildsøylen, komme ut og stå der hun var. Jeg vet én ting: jeg vet ikke nøyaktig hva som skjedde, men neste dag ble hun sendt hjem frisk. Det vet jeg.
Nå er det Hans vesen. Hvis vi leser Skriften, finner vi ut at det var en ildsøyle som ledet Israels barn. Den samme ildsøylen, tror jeg, var over Jesus Kristus. Jeg tror Han har stått opp fra de døde og viser de samme tegnene som Han viste da Han var her på jorden. Og det er den samme ildsøylen som er over menigheten akkurat nå. Den samme Guds Engel, den samme tilstedeværelsen.

text:
Now, there is times you say, "God, we want to look at it that way, but there's many times that things are done and said that angelic Beings brings the message to Him." God was in a certain place, and met a certain man at a certain time, and if He could have found a man one time, in the end He would have slew him, and so forth. We realize that there's times where God is certain places. That's exactly truth. The Scripture teaches that.
Now, in this case, when we promised God… And I told you a few nights ago about the healing of Florence Nightingale. When we went down into Africa… Now, my wife is sitting somewhere in the building. I haven't spied where she's at yet, but anyhow, I guess she's got back up in a corner. So, anyhow, she's as bashful as I used to be. And so, anyhow, when we were … Knowed so much about the … I tell you how much geography we knowed, I told her. She said, "Where you going now, honey."
text_no:
Det hender du sier: "Gud, vi ønsker å se på det på den måten, men ofte er det De engler som bringer budskapet til Ham." Gud var på et bestemt sted og møtte en bestemt mann til en bestemt tid. Dersom Han hadde funnet en mann en gang, ville Han til slutt ha drept ham, og så videre. Vi innser at det finnes tider der Gud er på bestemte steder. Det er helt sant. Skriften lærer oss det.
Når vi lovet Gud... Jeg fortalte dere for noen kvelder siden om helbredelsen av Florence Nightingale. Da vi dro ned til Afrika... Nå, min kone sitter et sted i bygningen. Jeg har ikke sett hvor hun er ennå, men jeg antar hun har funnet et hjørne. Hun er like sjenert som jeg pleide å være. Så, da vi visste så mye om... Jeg fortalte henne hvor mye geografi vi kjente til. Hun spurte meg: "Hvor skal du nå, kjære?"

text:
I said, "I'm going down to Durban, South Africa, or Durban, Southern Rhodesia. That's where I'm going." And she wrote … I said, "You write all of my mail to Durban, Southern Rhodesia." And here, she was writing to Durban, Southern Rhodesia, and Durban's in South Africa, not in Rhodesia. Just be like writing Owensboro, British Columbia. There is no such a place. See? But anyhow, the man, I guess, seeing how dumb we were, so they just … The post office sent it on down to South Africa--another nation down in Durban.
But when we landed in Johannesburg that afternoon, I guess there was many, many thousand people at the ramp. And so then, when we went out that night and got into Durban… Now, Brother Bosworth's sitting here, which was right present for every bit of it. And there was Mr. Baxter, and many of the others, my boy also was present. And when we landed, why then, they had to take me several miles out into the country where they had no place to put the people, and no park, no nothing in the city. Had to go way out into the country to where there's a big school, and place them out there. And I just the police was guarding the roads up and down. Just strings of people for miles. And there they was, all out in this place. It taken us, oh, I guess, twenty-five, thirty minutes to drive to where the place was.
text_no:
Jeg sa, "Jeg skal til Durban, Sør-Afrika, eller Durban, Sør-Rhodesia. Det er dit jeg skal." Hun skrev … Jeg sa, "Du sender all min post til Durban, Sør-Rhodesia." Men hun skrev til Durban, Sør-Rhodesia, mens Durban er i Sør-Afrika, ikke i Rhodesia. Det blir som å skrive Owensboro, British Columbia. Et slikt sted finnes ikke, skjønner? Men uansett, posten, som skjønte hvor forvirret vi var, sendte den til Sør-Afrika—til en annen nasjon, til Durban.
Da vi landet i Johannesburg den ettermiddagen, var det mange tusen mennesker ved rampen. Så da vi reiste videre til Durban den kvelden … Nå, Bror Bosworth sitter her, han var til stede gjennom alt sammen. Og Mr. Baxter og flere andre, inkludert sønnen min, var også til stede. Da vi landet, måtte de ta oss flere mil ut på landet fordi det ikke var plass til folk i byen, og ingen park, ingenting. Vi måtte dra langt ut på landet til en stor skole, hvor de plasserte folk. Politiet måtte vokte veiene opp og ned med lange rekker av folk i flere mil. Og der var de, samlet på dette stedet. Det tok oss, åh, cirka tjuefem til tretti minutter å kjøre dit.

text:
And when we got there, I'd been in the air for three days and three nights. Almost wrecked twice. You heard in the paper here about three years ago, when a car … Fifty-two were killed at one time. We were just behind that accident. And the same place, the instrument broke, and we were over that city, my boy and I, over that city for about two and a half hours trying to come down with no instrument, and it so foggy you couldn't even see nothing. It'd go right down real low and see the lights, then it'd go… My wife would have been screaming and crying and praying if she'd have known that I was in that fix then.
But not only was she praying, that she had been been praying, I was praying. That's right. And I was very thankful that I had a Heavenly Father who was riding by my side to help me down.
text_no:
Da vi kom dit, hadde jeg vært i luften i tre dager og tre netter. Vi hadde nesten hatt to ulykker. For omtrent tre år siden hørte du i nyhetene om en bilulykke hvor 52 mennesker mistet livet samtidig. Vi var akkurat bak den ulykken. På samme sted fikk vi instrumentfeil, og sønnen min og jeg fløy over byen i omkring to og en halv time uten instrumenter. Det var så tåkete at vi knapt kunne se noe som helst. Vi gikk veldig lavt for å se lysene, men så måtte vi stige igjen. Min kone ville ha skreket, grått og bedt hvis hun visste hvilken situasjon vi var i.
Men hun ba ikke bare da; hun hadde bedt hele tiden. Jeg ba også. Det stemmer. Jeg var svært takknemlig for at jeg hadde en Himmelsk Far som var ved min side og hjalp meg ned.

text:
Sailors and things, when they got off of there, said, "Well, this is the last one. For the rest of the way from here, we will go by ship. They can have all the airplanes they want. We'll take ours by water." And so, they were ready to get away.
And so, finally when we got in there, and got out to the place where the meeting was, I just got to the platform, tired, no sleep, you can imagine what it was, rocking and pitching in that air for all that time, and thousands of people, just like oceans of people were sitting. And they were … I was strange. I thought Africans were all black people, but every one of these was white. And I said, "Are these Africans?"
"Yeah, they're all Afrikaans people."
text_no:
Sjømennene sa da de gikk i land: "Dette var den siste turen med fly. For resten av reisen tar vi skip. De andre kan ha så mange fly de vil. Vi tar oss over vannet." De var klare til å komme seg videre.
Til slutt, da vi kom fram til stedet der møtet skulle holdes, var jeg utslitt etter ingen søvn og den turbulente flyturen. Tusenvis av mennesker, et hav av mennesker, satt og ventet. Jeg ble overrasket over at alle der var hvite, da jeg hadde trodd at afrikanere var svarte. Jeg spurte: "Er disse afrikanere?"
"Ja, de er alle afrikaanere," var svaret.

text:
So, I expect when I landed down there that I'd have to find a beaten path to the little huts, but I come to find out it's more modern than our cities are here in America. It's just a beautiful place, Johannesburg. So I thought, "My, this is strange." I thought, "Where's all the David Livingston's? They won't need them around here no more."
And so, we went out to the people there, and just as soon as I got to the platform, I happen to notice coming across the top of the people, I guess, Brother Bosworth, I hadn't been there, over, about five minutes, till a vision started, and I seen a bus moving like this over top the audience, and it was something blue, and I just kept watching it till it got to me. I wasn't saying nothing, because I was tired, and didn't know just exactly where to make my first beginning, the closing to a vision, to close in with the individual to a vision. And I noticed the bus coming. It come right close to me real close, and I seen a sign on it said, "Durban." And the bus went out of my sight. Well, I just started on speaking, and went on, and I noticed, here come that bus again. And I seen it stop. I seen a boy get on that had one leg about six inches shorter than the other one on a crutch. And he held his leg on this crutch, you see, and walked like that. He got on the bus, and I seen him coming on like that. That bus come on up close to me and stopped again. Well, I just kept on speaking about said, "I'm awfully tired tonight. I'm really worn out." I said, "We're expecting a great meeting here in Africa. We just closed one in New York City."
Now, there's something about New York City. You watch. Remember, there's something about New York City. Don't forget that, you hear? All right. I had just left New York, New York. And I said, "I'm so tired." And I watched that. Then I seen this boy again coming, walking across the audience and he didn't have this crutch under his arm anymore. He was just normal and well, with his leg just a normal size.
text_no:
Da jeg forventet å lande der nede, trodde jeg at jeg måtte finne en sti til de små hyttene, men jeg fant ut at Johannesburg er mer moderne enn byene våre her i Amerika. Det er et vakkert sted. Jeg tenkte: "Dette er merkelig." og "Hvor er alle David Livingstone'ene? De vil ikke trenge dem her lenger."
Vi dro ut til folket der, og så snart jeg kom på plattformen, la jeg merke til, jeg tror det var Bror Bosworth, at etter bare fem minutter begynte en visjon. Jeg så en blå buss bevege seg over publikum, og jeg fulgte med på den til den kom mot meg. Jeg sa ingenting fordi jeg var sliten og visste ikke helt når jeg skulle begynne med å avslutte en visjon og gå inn på en individuell visjon. Jeg la merke til at bussen kom veldig nærme og jeg så et skilt hvor det sto "Durban". Deretter forsvant bussen ut av syne.
Jeg fortsatte å snakke, men så kom bussen tilbake. Denne gangen så jeg en gutt med et bein som var omtrent seks tommer kortere enn det andre, bruke en krykke. Han holdt beinet på krykken mens han gikk og steg på bussen. Bussen kom nær meg igjen og stoppet. Jeg fortsatte å tale og sa: "Jeg er veldig sliten i kveld. Jeg er virkelig utslitt. Vi forventer et stort møte her i Afrika. Vi avsluttet nettopp et møte i New York City."
Det er noe med New York City. Husk det, hør? Jeg sa: "Jeg har nettopp forlatt New York og jeg er så sliten." Jeg så igjen gutten komme gående gjennom publikum, men nå uten krykken under armen. Han var normal og frisk, og beinet hans var av normal størrelse.

text:
Now, this is the first thing, they'd been sitting there. I'd been in the audience of people at least ten minutes. And I happened to look, and where the boy walked and stopped in the air, I looked right down below him, and there sat the boy, just about the distance of this building, hardly as far. And I looked and seen the boy. And I said, "Don't you come from Durban there, son, with the white shirt on?"
He said, "Yes, sir." Spoke English. "Yes, sir." He come from Durban.
I said, "Aren't one of your legs about six inches shorter than the other?"
And he said, "That's me, exactly."
I said, "It isn't anymore. You're healed." And here he come walking up there just as normal just as he could be. And his people begin screaming, and faith, oh, my, with one collapse.
text_no:
Dette er den første tingen: De hadde sittet der. Jeg hadde vært i publikum i minst ti minutter. Så skjedde det at jeg fikk øye på gutten som gikk og stoppet opp i luften. Jeg så rett nedenfor ham, og der satt gutten, omtrent på samme avstand som denne bygningen, knapt så langt. Jeg så gutten og sa: "Kommer du ikke fra Durban, sønn, med den hvite skjorten?"
Han sa: "Ja, sir." Han snakket engelsk. "Ja, sir." Han kom fra Durban.
Jeg sa: "Er ikke det ene beinet ditt omtrent seks tommer kortere enn det andre?"
Han sa: "Det stemmer akkurat."
Jeg sa: "Det er det ikke lenger. Du er helbredet." Og der kom han gående opp så normal som han kunne være. Folkene hans begynte å skrike, og troen, å, min, med ett kraftig utbrudd.

text:
Just then I seen a little green car hit the road like this, and turn around like this, and slide backward, hit a tree. I seen them take a young lady, blonde-headed, and she was all broke up. They wouldn't move her. They went and got the doctors, and they put her on a ambulance. It was in her spine. She was broke three or four places in the spine. But I seen the thing take place, but I couldn't tell where it was. I couldn't see that girl. It went away. There was a high bench like a high place built, and I happened to look right down under me, here she laid right down here, beneath right here. I seen it was the same girl. And I said, "Didn't you have an accident recently?"
"Yes."
And I said, "You was in a little green car, and the car slid sideways and then backed up like this and hit a tree."
She said, "That's right."
I said, "Your back is broke."
And her mother said, "Oh, she just can't move."
I looked out, and I seen the girl going real fast right down across the audience in a vision, going walking real fast. I said, "Stand up, in the name of Jesus Christ, for "Thus saith the Lord," you're healed."
text_no:
Akkurat da så jeg en liten grønn bil kollidere og svinge rundt, for deretter å skli bakover og treffe et tre. Jeg så at de tok ut en ung dame med blondt hår, og hun var skadet. De flyttet henne ikke. De hentet legene, og de la henne inn i en ambulanse. Det var i ryggraden - hun hadde brudd tre eller fire steder i ryggraden. Jeg så det hele skje, men kunne ikke se nøyaktig hvor det var. Jeg kunne ikke se jenta selv. Synet forsvant. Det var en høy benk eller et slags høyt sted bygget, og plutselig oppdaget jeg at hun lå rett under meg. Jeg skjønte det var den samme jenta.
Jeg spurte, "Hadde du en ulykke nylig?"
"Ja," svarte hun.
Jeg sa, "Du var i en liten grønn bil som skled sidelengs, rykket bakover og traff et tre."
Hun bekreftet, "Det stemmer."
Jeg sa, "Ryggen din er brukket."
Moren hennes utbrøt, "Åh, hun kan ikke bevege seg."
Jeg så ut over forsamlingen og så jenta i en visjon, gående raskt. Jeg sa, "Reis deg, i Jesu Kristi navn, for 'Slik sier Herren,' du er helbredet."

text:
And she said… Her mother said, "Oh, no, darling. No, no." Said, "If she moves, she will die. The doctor said she'd die." And the girl jumped up and let out a big scream, and her mother fainted and fell in the same cot. So they just let her lay in the cot, and let the girl go on. Is that right, Brother Bosworth? [Brother Bosworth says, "Amen. Amen."]
Yes, sure.
["The next night I saw this, her back was broke in three places. I saw her in the audience the next night, and waited till I got her eye, to come up here. And she came up and sat. I told her, 'Sit on one of these seats back here till Brother Branham comes. I want to introduce you.' And so she sat there, and as quick as I saw you come through that side door. 'Come quick, come quick!" And there they took the picture. We have the picture now."] That's right. Thank you, brother.
text_no:
Hun fortalte det til moren, som sa: «Å nei, kjære. Nei, nei.» Hun sa: «Hvis hun rører seg, vil hun dø. Legen sa at hun ville dø.» Men jenta spratt opp og skrek høyt, og moren hennes besvimte og falt på samme seng. Så de lot henne bare ligge der, og lot jenta fortsette. 
Er det riktig, Bror Bosworth? [Bror Bosworth sier: «Amen. Amen.»]
Ja, absolutt.
[Neste kveld så jeg jenta, hennes rygg var brukket på tre steder. Jeg så henne i publikum og ventet til jeg fikk øyekontakt med henne, for å få henne til å komme opp hit. Hun kom opp og satte seg. Jeg sa til henne: «Sett deg på en av disse setene her bak til Bror Branham kommer. Jeg vil introdusere deg.» Og hun satt der, og med en gang jeg så deg komme inn gjennom sidedøren, sa jeg: «Kom raskt, kom raskt!» Da tok de bildet. Vi har bildet nå.] Det stemmer. Takk, bror.

text:
Her mother fainted and fell in the same cot. So, they just pushed it over, and let her mother lay in the cot while the girl rejoiced in the Lord going up and down the place. And a doctor said, "If she moved…" And you know what it is, a broken back, an accident on a highway here. Even a … No one can move that person unless the doctor orders it. And you better know what your speaking about before you call such a person, too. If that's in your own head, well, something will… The person will kill themselves. But don't worry. If God says so, you move. That's just exactly, because it will be that way.
Well, we were just… Oh, there's so many things happened there in about fifteen minutes, till I just collapsed almost. They taken me to the house. And on the road up, there was a man, Brother Schoeman and them, many of you may know him. He was the chairman of the National Committee. And all the churches of Africa was cooperating. All but the Dutch Reform Church, and that's really a hard church to get along with. If there's any here, I don't mean to say any word about the church, but they don't believe in water baptism, they don't believe anything hardly. And so, they are just a very formal, ritualistic, cold, indifferent church.
text_no:
Hennes mor besvimte og falt i samme seng. Så de skjøv bare sengen til siden og lot moren ligge mens jenta frydet seg i Herren, løpende opp og ned. En lege sa: "Hvis hun beveget seg..." Du vet hva det betyr, en brukket rygg, en ulykke på en motorvei her. Ingen kan røre den personen med mindre legen gir ordre om det. Og du må vite nøyaktig hva du snakker om før du kaller på en slik person. Hvis det bare er i ditt eget hode, vel, noe kan gå galt… Personen kan skade seg selv alvorlig. Men ikke bekymre deg. Hvis Gud sier det, da handler du. Så enkelt er det, for det vil skje slik.
Vel, vi var bare… Å, det skjedde så mange ting der i løpet av omtrent femten minutter at jeg nesten kollapset. De tok meg med til huset. På vei opp var det en mann, Bror Schoeman og andre. Mange av dere kjenner kanskje ham. Han var leder av nasjonalkomiteen. Alle menighetene i Afrika samarbeidet, bortsett fra den hollandske reformerte kirken, og den er virkelig en vanskelig kirke å samarbeide med. Hvis noen her tilhører den kirken, mener jeg ikke å si noe negativt om menigheten, men de tror verken på dåp eller stort annet. De er en veldig formell, ritualistisk, kald og likegyldig kirke.

text:
And so the next day, Brother Schoeman going up, which he belonged to the Apostolic Faith Missions of Africa. And he was the President of my group. And he was a National Committee. And Brother Schoeman, going home that night, he said, "Oh, I think that was wonderful." But he was telling me that, but yet, I knowed he didn't believe that. I knowed he didn't believe it. And I was going home at his house to stay all night, because there was no places to stay. So, I went to his house to stay all night, and we went in the room, Brother Baxter said, "Oh," said, "I tell you, this fellow Schoeman is a mighty man."
I said, "Yes, he is." But I knowed he wasn't believing that.
text_no:
Neste dag dro Bror Schoeman, som tilhørte Apostolic Faith Missions of Africa. Han var president i min gruppe og medlem av nasjonalkomiteen. Bror Schoeman dro hjem den kvelden og sa: "Å, jeg synes det var fantastisk." Han fortalte meg det, men jeg visste at han ikke trodde på det. Jeg visste at han ikke trodde på det. Jeg skulle overnatte hos ham fordi det ikke var andre steder å bo. Så jeg dro til hans hus for å overnatte, og da vi kom inn i rommet, sa Bror Baxter: "Å, jeg sier deg, denne Schoeman er en mektig mann."
Jeg svarte: "Ja, han er det." Men jeg visste at han ikke trodde på det.

text:
So, the next morning come out … He was a nice man, but he thought sure that it was a mental telepathy, or some kind of a trick I had up my sleeve to do it. It was like a rabbits foot or something, you know, you rub on you. So, I knowed he was suspicious. And the next morning, I got up, and we went to breakfast. And when we was setting at the breakfast table, I thought … I looked at him again, and caught him this way to know that I'd catch his spirit. I said, "It's beautiful weather in Africa, Brother Schoeman."
Said, "Yes, yes very, very, very."
text_no:
Neste morgen kom han ut... Han var en hyggelig mann, men han var sikker på at det jeg gjorde var mental telepati eller en slags triks jeg hadde i ermet. Det var som en lykkebringer, noe du gnir på deg. Jeg visste at han var mistenksom. Neste morgen sto jeg opp, og vi gikk til frokost. Da vi satt ved frokostbordet, tenkte jeg... Jeg så på ham igjen og fanget hans ånd på denne måten. Jeg sa, "Det er vakkert vær i Afrika, Bror Schoeman."
Han svarte, "Ja, ja, veldig, veldig."

text:
And I thought, "Uh-huh." You could just feel that little shadow there. I knew that he wasn't just exactly where he should be. So, I thought, "Now, Lord, if You will just help me to just kind of shake him just a little, then it will make the thing … Because he's the chairman of the committee." I thought, "Well, if You don't, why, it's all right. Just let it go."
So, I was sitting there eating, and after a while the Holy Spirit come down and there broke a vision across the table while we were sitting there. I said, "Brother Schoeman, your little girl here, isn't her name Andria?"
He said, "Yes."
And I said, "About six months ago, you almost lost her, didn't you?"
He said, "Yes, sir."
I said, "It was something wrong in her throat."
Said, "Yes, sir."
I said, "She's had a hard time swallowing since then, hasn't she?"
Said, "Say, that's right."
text_no:
Jeg tenkte, "Åh, ja." Jeg kunne bare merke den lille skyggen der. Jeg visste at han ikke helt var der han burde være. Så jeg tenkte, "Nå, Herre, hvis Du bare vil hjelpe meg med å riste litt tak i ham, så vil det gjøre saken ... Fordi han er formann i komiteen." Jeg tenkte, "Vel, hvis Du ikke gjør det, er det greit. La det være."
Jeg satt der og spiste, og etter en stund kom Den Hellige Ånd ned, og en visjon dukket opp på bordet mens vi satt der. Jeg sa, "Bror Schoeman, din lille jente her, heter hun ikke Andria?"
Han svarte, "Ja."
Jeg sa, "For omtrent seks måneder siden holdt du nesten på å miste henne, gjorde du ikke?"
Han svarte, "Ja, sir."
Jeg sa, "Det var noe galt med halsen hennes."
Han sa, "Ja, sir."
Jeg sa, "Hun har hatt vanskeligheter med å svelge siden da, ikke sant?"
Han sa, "Ja, det er riktig."

text:
And then it turned around and told him something that I won't say here, because some his friends may be sitting here. And he just almost passed out at the table.
He said, "Brother Branham, I told Brother Baxter before we come in." He said, "Brother Branham, I was just a little bit skeptic, but I want to reach my hand to you." It was clear then. He knew.
So that day, we had a marvelous time. The next morning, Brother Baxter and all of us, we come in and he said, "Brother Branham," he said, "I got some news for you."
Said, "What?"
text_no:
Og så snudde det seg og sa noe til ham som jeg ikke vil gjenta her, fordi noen av vennene hans kan være til stede. Han ble nesten sjokkert ved bordet.
Han sa: "Bror Branham, jeg fortalte Bror Baxter før vi kom inn." Han fortsatte: "Bror Branham, jeg var litt skeptisk, men nå vil jeg rekke ut hånden til deg." Da ble det klart. Han forsto.
Den dagen hadde vi en fantastisk tid. Neste morgen kom Bror Baxter og vi andre inn, og han sa: "Bror Branham, jeg har noen nyheter til deg."
Jeg spurte: "Hva?"

text:
He said, "How would you like to go way down through Kimberly and Cape Town, and all around through there, went up around through Victoria?"
"Oh," I said, "that would be marvelous."
He said, "Do you know what? Brother DuPlessis has come over here and set up an itinerary, all of them, and we're going to take a great big itinerary hundreds of miles down through here."
I said, "Marvelous! Marvelous! That's just fine."
So then, after the service that night, I went home, went in the room, went to bed, went to sleep, and about two o'clock in the morning I woke up, and I heard some kind of a little old bird out the window. And I slipped over and raised up and looked through those reeds to look where the birds was. And it was chirping at night. And I thought, "That isn't a nightingale." And I looked at my watch, and it was about two o'clock. I come back and set down, turned on a little dim light and begin to read my Bible. And just then, I looked up and here He was standing before me, milling around.
text_no:
Han sa: "Hva sier du til å reise ned gjennom Kimberly og Cape Town og rundt der, og opp gjennom Victoria?"
"Å," sa jeg, "det hadde vært fantastisk."
Han sa: "Vet du hva? Bror DuPlessis har kommet hit og satt opp en reiserute, og vi skal dekke en stor reiserute på hundrevis av mil."
Jeg svarte: "Fantastisk! Fantastisk! Det høres helt greit ut."
Etter møtet den kvelden dro jeg hjem, gikk inn på rommet, la meg og sovnet. Rundt klokken to om morgenen våknet jeg av lyden fra en liten fugl utenfor vinduet. Jeg gikk bort og kikket gjennom sivet for å se hvor fuglen var. Den kvitret om natten, og jeg tenkte: "Det er ikke en nattergal." Jeg sjekket klokken; den var rundt to. Jeg satte meg ned, slo på et svakt lys og begynte å lese Bibelen min. Plutselig så jeg Ham stående foran meg, bevegende rundt.

text:
He said, "Don't you go with them, but you stay right here in Durban." And said, "Right here in Johannesburg." Said, "You stay here two weeks, and then there is going to be a man by the name of Jackson, is going to take you lion hunting. And you're going out, and you take that week with him and go out and hunt lions, and tigers, and things for a week out in the jungle." Said, "Come back, and then you go over to Durban. A place called Durban, and stay there a month."
He said, "Now that you might know that this will come to pass," he said, "tomorrow morning," he said, "they're going to take you down to this city. And there's going to be a little garden like, and a native standing there with a purple shirt on." Said, "It's going to be a rare thing. You tell the men that they're going to take you down there." And said, "Then they're going to get a doctor, and they're going to work it around through another minister, and this doctor is going to be a thin man wearing glasses." And said, "The doctors tell him that he's got cancer and dying." But said, "It's an error. It's an error of the doctors. They cut the wrong thing in him, and he's dying because of that." And said, "Don't pray for him, for he's going to die."
text_no:
Han sa, "Du skal ikke dra med dem, men bli her i Durban." Og sa, "Her i Johannesburg." Sa, "Du skal være her i to uker, og deretter kommer en mann ved navn Jackson til å ta deg med på løvejakt. Du skal dra ut og jakte på løver, tigre og andre dyr i en uke ute i jungelen." Sa, "Kom tilbake, og deretter reiser du til Durban. Et sted kalt Durban, og du blir der en måned."
Han sa, "For at du skal vite at dette vil skje," sa han, "i morgen tidlig," sa han, "de skal ta deg ned til denne byen. Og der vil det være en liten hage, og en innfødt som står der med en lilla skjorte." Sa, "Det blir en sjelden hendelse. Du skal fortelle mennene at de skal ta deg dit." Og sa, "Deretter skal de finne en lege, og de skal ordne det via en annen forkynner, og denne legen kommer til å være en tynn mann med briller." Og sa, "Legene sier at han har kreft og holder på å dø." Men sa, "Det er en feil. Det er en feil fra legene. De opererte på feil sted i ham, og han dør på grunn av det." Og sa, "Ikke be for ham, for han kommer til å dø."

text:
And He said, "Now, and on the road, they're going to take you to another city. And on the road, you're going to meet a girl. She will be selling beads on the side of the road. She's going to have a great big skin place on the side of her head. And while you're standing there," said, "Mr. Baxter is going to call your attention to a very funny looking big bird making a noise as it goes across the plain." And said, "By this, you will know, and they will know that you're not to take that itinerary that them ministers has got set up, but you're to go this way."
Well, that settled it. So, I went in, woke Brother Baxter up. I said, "Brother Baxter, we can't take that itinerary."
Said, "Well, you will have to tell Brother Schoeman tomorrow."
And I told Brother Schoeman. Oh, if you've ever been in Africa, why, that's kind of hard to let them know that, you know.
text_no:
Og Han sa: "Nå, på veien skal de føre deg til en annen by. Underveis vil du møte en jente som selger perler langs veien. Hun vil ha et stort område med skadet hud på siden av hodet. Mens du står der vil Mr. Baxter påpeke en merkelig fugl som lager lyd mens den krysser sletten." Han sa: "Ved dette vil du og de forstå at du ikke skal følge den planen som prestene har lagt opp, men at du skal gå denne veien."
Da var det avgjort. Jeg gikk inn og vekket Bror Baxter. Jeg sa: "Bror Baxter, vi kan ikke følge den reiseruten."
Han sa: "Da må du fortelle det til Bror Schoeman i morgen."
Så jeg fortalte det til Bror Schoeman. Åh, hvis du noen gang har vært i Afrika, vet du at det kan være vanskelig å formidle slik informasjon.

text:
Said, "Oh, it's set up." So here come the whole association. That night, the Lord worked a powerful miracle. And the next morning, when we got up, they'd … I will tell you what the miracle was.
As the second night there--third night, it would have been that night, and here's what taken place. Now, this Afrikaans man, this Boer had never heard about the Angel of the Lord. Now we got the shirt, and everything to show, the papers and everything, headlines two or three pages, in the Dutch Reform church which controls. And the second morning, while we were there, I forget to tell you this: The first night I was there, and the first night I spoke and they seen the miracle, there happened to be a group of the Medical Association out there, and the next morning, the entire Medical Association of all South Africa, called me to come to breakfast and give me the right-hand of fellowship, and said, "Brother Branham, that's genuine divine healing."
text_no:
Sa: "Å, det er arrangert." Så kom hele foreningen. Den kvelden utførte Herren et kraftfullt mirakel. Neste morgen, da vi sto opp, hadde de … Jeg skal fortelle dere hva miraklet var.
Dette skjedde på den tredje natten. Denne afrikaaner-mannen, denne boeren, hadde aldri hørt om Herrens Engel. Vi har skjorten og alt for å vise, avisene og alt, overskrifter over to-tre sider i den Nederlandske Reformerte kirken som har kontrollen. Den andre morgenen, mens vi var der, glemte jeg å fortelle dere dette: Første kvelden jeg var der, og første kvelden jeg talte, så de miraklet. Det var en gruppe fra den medisinske foreningen der, og neste morgen ba hele den medisinske foreningen i hele Sør-Afrika meg komme til frokost. De gav meg høyrehånds fellesskap og sa: "Bror Branham, det er ekte guddommelig helbredelse."

text: How about that, Brother Bosworth? Huh? "The genuine divine healing, which we're absolutely not against." He said, "Now, there's many people down here that didn't believe in doctors. We heard you speak for the doctors." Said, "There's been people that we could have helped, Rev. Branham, and we are Christians." He said, "We could have helped them, but they had the Christian Science idea that you shouldn't have a doctor." And said, "Through that, they died, many of them. And we believe we could have helped them." And said, "Now, they love you. And you spoke for us," and said, "now, we're speaking for you, and anything that we can do for you, let us know, and every hospital in the whole Transvaal, Free State, Orange State, everything's absolutely open. Anybody that can come, you can go to any hospital. Any thing we can do, we will be right along with you to help you." And they followed me throughout entire Africa. And in a few moments, I will get to the story of the conversion of some of them, and if I can hurry to get my time in, now. All right. Or get my story out before my time is up.
text_no: Hvordan er det med det, Bror Bosworth? "Den ekte guddommelige helbredelsen, som vi absolutt ikke er imot." Han sa: "Nå, det finnes mange mennesker her nede som ikke trodde på leger. Vi hørte du talte til fordel for legene." Han sa: "Det har vært mennesker vi kunne ha hjulpet, Rev. Branham, og vi er kristne." Han sa: "Vi kunne ha hjulpet dem, men de hadde den kristne vitenskapelige ideen om at man ikke skulle ha en lege." Og han sa: "På grunn av det døde mange av dem. Og vi tror vi kunne ha hjulpet dem." Han sa: "Nå elsker de deg. Og du snakket for oss," og sa, "nå, vi snakker for deg. Alt vi kan gjøre for deg, bare la oss vite det, og hvert sykehus i hele Transvaal, Free State, Orange State, alt er helt åpent. Hvem som helst kan komme, du kan dra til ethvert sykehus. Hva vi enn kan gjøre, vi vil være der for å hjelpe deg." Og de fulgte meg gjennom hele Afrika. Om noen få øyeblikk vil jeg komme til historien om omvendelsen av noen av dem, hvis jeg rekker å få med meg alt. Greit. Eller få historien ut før tiden min er oppbrukt.

text:
So then, that same night, there was one of these Boer ministers. That's a church of the Boer. They called the Dutch Reform church, they went out. He went out to see his friend, and his friend said that I was a spiritualist, mind reader. So he went over to his friend, he said, "God have mercy on you." Said, "It's the hour of visitation to Africa. And you doing such a thing as that." Said, "That's sin."
He said, "The man ain't nothing in the world but a polished up soothsayer."
And he said, "I'd go out and have prayer for your sinful soul, because God might strike you dead." And he goes out in the kneels down there, in a little, I believe it was a peach tree. A little peach tree just about twenty yards from the house, raised up his hands. They wear a white shirt, and a white tie. And so, he raised up his hands, said, "God, have mercy on my friend in there. He's going to die and go to hell with such thoughts as that." And said, "The hour of visitation is here, and look at him in there. There he is sitting in there." Oh, he was just telling God all about it. Now here's the man's story. He said that a Pillar of Light, about two foot long, about eight inches, or ten inches high, of a Light came down and the Light parted from side to side, and that man described that Angel to a dot. Said, a large Man, two hundred pounds, dark hair to His shoulder, walked down to him and laid His hand on his back. And he said, "When He laid his hand on his back, it felt like that fire burnt plumb through his body." And he said, "Rise, and tell your friend that this is the hour of visitation, and not to condemn this man."
text_no:
Samme natt var det en av disse boer-predikantene. Det er en menighet av boere, og de kalte det den nederlandske reformerte kirke. Han gikk ut for å møte en venn, og vennen sa at jeg var en spiritist og tankeleser. Så han dro til vennen sin og sa: "Gud, ha miskunn med deg. Dette er timen for besøket til Afrika, og du gjør slikt som det der." Han sa, "Det er synd."
Vennen svarte: "Den mannen er ikke noe annet enn en polert spåmann."
Predikanten sa: "Jeg skal be for din syndige sjel, fordi Gud kan slå deg død." Så han gikk ut til en liten ferskentre, omtrent tjue meter fra huset. Han knelte ned, løftet hendene og ba: "Gud, ha miskunn med min venn der inne. Han vil dø og havne i helvete med slike tanker." Han fortsatte med å si, "Timens besøket er her, og se på ham som sitter der inne." Han fortalte alt til Gud.
Ifølge predikantens beretning, så han en lyssøyle, omtrent to fot lang og åtte til ti tommer høy, stige ned. Lyset delte seg fra side til side, og mannen beskrev engelen i detalj. Han sa det var en stor mann, rundt nitti kilo, med mørkt hår til skuldrene. Engelen la hånden på ryggen hans, og predikanten følte det som ild brenne gjennom kroppen. Engelen sa, "Reis deg og fortell vennen din at dette er timen for besøket, og ikke fordøm denne mannen."

text:
So, he jumped up from there, and he said, "Did I go to sleep? Was I dreaming?" And he run and said, "Well, well, I don't…" And he run up and begin to tell his friend about it, and his friend looked around, and here was the shape of that Angel's hand scorched in that white, broad-clothed shirt. And the next morning, the man fainted, and they took it, and here was a picture that big in the paper. Went plumb through all Africa.
I got the shirt, now. They called me down, the authorities, checked my hand with it, and every… And there the man's hand, just perfectly a man's hand scorched right through his shirt. We got the shirt. There it was in the African paper. Right a way? My! And then, they wanted me to leave. Why, I said, "No! The Lord told me stay here." He said for me to stay here.
The next day, when we started out, everything the Lord had said would take place, took place just exactly that way. Even to the little bead salesman, Brother Baxter trying to take her picture. I said, "Don't you remember that beads salesman I told you?"
text_no:
Han hoppet opp og spurte: "Har jeg sovnet? Drømte jeg?" Deretter løp han og sa: "Vel, vel, jeg vet ikke..." Han løp opp og begynte å fortelle vennen sin om det. Vennen så seg rundt, og der var avtrykket av engelens hånd brent inn i den hvite skjorten av bredklede. Neste morgen besvimte mannen, og bildet ble publisert i avisen, så stort som dette. Det spredte seg over hele Afrika.
Jeg har skjorten nå. Myndighetene kontaktet meg, og de sjekket hånden min mot merket på skjorten. Håndavtrykket var perfekt synlig, brent rett gjennom skjorten. Vi har skjorten fortsatt, og bildet var i den afrikanske avisen med én gang. For en opplevelse! De ønsket at jeg skulle forlate stedet, men jeg sa: "Nei! Herren ba meg bli her." Han sa at jeg skulle bli her.
Dagen etter, da vi startet, skjedde alt Herren hadde sagt ville skje, akkurat slik. Til og med den lille perleselgeren, da Bror Baxter prøvde å ta et bilde av henne, sa jeg: "Husker du ikke perleselgeren jeg fortalte deg om?"

text:
"Oh," he said, "Brother Branham, that's her." Just then something went, "Squawk, squawk." And it was a wild peacock, going flying down through … he said, "Look at that bird there. Wasn't that a funny looking thing, Brother Branham?"
I said, "It is."
Brother Schoeman said, "That's a wild peacock."
And I said, "Do you remember, Brother Baxter, what I told?"
He said, "Well, Brother Branham, it's exactly, isn't it?"
And I said, "Brother Schoeman, I can't take that itinerary." I said, "I'm sorry to have to interfere with you ministers, but He told me not to take it."
Said, "Brother Branham, we have to take it."
"Oh," I said, "but, you may have to take it, but not me." I said, "No, I don't have to take it, Brother Schoeman."
text_no:
"Oh," sa han, "Bror Branham, det er henne." Akkurat da hørte vi et "Skvauk, skvauk." Det var en vill påfugl som fløy nedover. Han sa, "Se på den fuglen der. Var ikke det en morsom skapning, Bror Branham?"
Jeg svarte, "Ja, det er det."
Bror Schoeman sa, "Det er en vill påfugl."
Jeg sa, "Husker du, Bror Baxter, hva jeg fortalte?"
Han svarte, "Vel, Bror Branham, det er akkurat som du sa, ikke sant?"
Jeg sa, "Bror Schoeman, jeg kan ikke ta den reiseruten." Jeg sa, "Jeg beklager å måtte forstyrre dere predikanter, men Han fortalte meg å ikke ta den."
Han sa, "Bror Branham, vi må ta den."
"Å," sa jeg, "dere må kanskje ta den, men ikke jeg." Jeg sa, "Nei, jeg må ikke ta den, Bror Schoeman."

text:
The next morning, just that persistent, here come the cars right up to go anyhow. Well, Brother Baxter said, "Are you going, Brother Branham?"
I said, "No, sir."
So, here they was outside, and my suitcase wasn't packed, or nothing. I said, "No. The Lord told me not to go."
Well, in come the ministers, and Brother Bosworth knows, we had a round of it. He said, "Well, the meetings are closed here."
And I said, "Ridiculous. Right where the Lord's give us the favor and everything, and telling us to stay here, here's where we're to stay."
text_no:
Neste morgen, like vedvarende, kom bilene rett opp for å dra uansett. Bror Baxter sa, "Skal du dra, Bror Branham?"
Jeg svarte, "Nei, sir."
Så der stod de utenfor, og kofferten min var ikke pakket, ingenting. Jeg sa, "Nei. Herren sa til meg at jeg ikke skulle dra."
Da kom forkynnerne, og Bror Bosworth vet, vi hadde en runde med det. Han sa, "Møtene er avsluttet her."
Jeg svarte, "Ubegripelig. Akkurat her hvor Herren har gitt oss favør og alt, og sier oss å bli her, er det her vi skal være."

text:
And no, they wanted to go. And so, I put my clothes under my arm and so forth, and we got out, Brother Bosworth and all of them right along. And we started down, and as we started going, just a hundred and something miles, I said, "What are you going down here for?"
"Well, we promised Brother So-and-so we'd be here."
text_no:
De ønsket virkelig å dra, så jeg tok klærne under armen og dro av gårde med Bror Bosworth og resten av dem. Da vi hadde kjørt omtrent et par hundre kilometer, spurte jeg: "Hva er grunnen til at vi drar hit?"
"Vi lovte Bror Hva-heter-han-at vi skulle være her," svarte de.

text: I say this with reverence brother, if you want to get in trouble, just mess with a preacher. You sure get in trouble. So, that's right. Now, they're God's servants. They have to be a little suspicious and things. But brother, I've had more trouble of preachers getting me out of the will of God than anything I've ever seen in my life." Yes, sir! Because one will say, "The Lord…" See, the man's right. If the Lord told him to do that, let him go do it. He may tell me something else. And no matter whatever tells you, if he's a prophet, if he's a preacher, whatever he may be, if God's told you something contrary, don't you pay no attention to that prophet, preacher, priest, or whatever it is, you listen to God. Always listen to God.
text_no: Jeg sier dette med respekt, Bror. Hvis du ønsker å havne i trøbbel, er det bare å tukle med en forkynner. Du vil definitivt få trøbbel. Det stemmer. De er Guds tjenere og må være litt mistenksomme av og til. Men, Bror, jeg har hatt mer trøbbel med forkynnere som får meg ut av Guds vilje enn noe annet jeg har opplevd i mitt liv. Ja, sir! For én vil si: "Herren..." Se, mannen har rett. Hvis Herren har sagt til ham å gjøre noe, la ham gjøre det. Han kan ha sagt noe annet til meg. Det spiller ingen rolle hva noen sier til deg, om han er profet, forkynner eller hva som helst; hvis Gud har sagt noe annet til deg, ikke bry Deg om den profeten, forkynneren eller presten. Hør på Gud. Alltid hør på Gud.

text:
How many ever read First Kings 13, where a little prophet went down and prophesied, and done works of God told him something to do. And another real prophet come to him and told him, said, "The Lord met me and said you come to my house and eat." You know what happened, don't you? Yes, sir. You listen to God, regardless of who says, or what says. You listen to God.
Well, the minister said, "We promised our brethren that we would bring you to their town."
I said, "What size city is this we're going to?"
"About three thousand."
"Why there was ten, fifteen, twenty thousand people out there to go to the meeting. Where are you going to put them?"
"Don't know. But we promised brother we'd be there." Ha. There you are.
I said, "I--I appreciate that. I love our brethren, but God has said. [Unclear word] to be right over there."
text_no:
Har noen lest Første Kongebok 13, der en liten profet gikk ned og profeterte, og gjorde Guds verk etter å ha fått instruksjoner? Og en annen profet kom til ham og sa: "Herren møtte meg og sa at du skulle komme til mitt hus og spise." Du vet hva som skjedde, ikke sant? Ja, sir. Du må lytte til Gud, uansett hvem som sier hva. Lytt til Gud.
Forkynneren sa: "Vi lovet brødrene våre at vi skulle ta deg med til byen deres."
Jeg spurte: "Hvor stor er byen vi drar til?"
"Omtrent tre tusen."
"Det var ti, femten, tjue tusen mennesker som ville komme til møtet. Hvor skal dere få plass til dem?"
"Vet ikke. Men vi lovet Bror å være der." Ha. Der har du det.
Jeg sa: "Jeg--jeg setter pris på det. Jeg elsker våre brødre, men Gud har sagt at jeg skal være akkurat der."

text:
So, we started down the road, and Brother and Sister Schoeman in the car, and Billy Paul, I believe … No, I was by myself, and I think Billy was back with Brother Bosworth and them. Three or four carloads of ministers coming, kept going on down, the Holy Spirit said, "Are you going to listen to Me?"
I said, "Brother Schoeman, stop the car."
He said, "What's the matter, Brother Branham?"
I said, "Just stop."
And he stopped and said, "Well, what?" We was way ahead of the rest of them, because he drove like Jehu. So, he pulled the brake up and he said, "Well, what's the matter?"
I said, "I'm just not going any farther."
"Well," he said, "Brother Branham," said, "I don't care for you staying up there." Said, He's the chairman. Said, "That's my city. That's my district." He said, "But, the brethren want you to go down there." He said, "They're not going to listen to that, Brother Branham."
I said, "Well, I'm going to listen to God, anyhow."
text_no:
Vi startet nedover veien med Bror og Søster Schoeman i bilen, og Billy Paul, tror jeg ... Nei, jeg var alene, og jeg tror Billy var bak med Bror Bosworth og dem. Tre eller fire billass med forkynnere fulgte etter. Mens vi fortsatte, spurte Den Hellige Ånd: "Vil du lytte til Meg?"
Jeg sa: "Bror Schoeman, stopp bilen."
Han svarte: "Hva er i veien, Bror Branham?"
Jeg sa: "Bare stopp."
Han stanset bilen og spurte: "Hva er i veien?" Vi var langt foran de andre, fordi han kjørte raskt som Jehu. Han trakk opp bremsen og sa: "Hva er problemet?"
Jeg sa: "Jeg skal ikke kjøre videre."
Han sa: "Bror Branham, jeg bryr meg ikke om du blir der oppe. Jeg er formannen. Dette er min by, mitt distrikt. Men, brødrene vil at du skal dra ned dit. De kommer ikke til å like dette, Bror Branham."
Jeg svarte: "Vel, jeg skal lytte til Gud uansett."

text:
So, he stopped, and up come the brethren. Now, if anybody's here remembers at Shreveport, Louisiana, standing speaking like this, one day the Holy Spirit come just before going over, and said, "Thus saith the Lord," to me, "be careful, there's a trap for you in Africa."
Well, I thought it was witch doctors and so forth. I thought, "Well, I know who God is, and witch doctors ain't going to bother me." I didn't know it was among brethren. See? So sly was Satan.
And we stopped on the road. Up come Brother Bosworth and Brother Baxter, and all of them stopped, and all them … So, Brother Schoeman went back there and said, "You will have to go see him." Said, "He just refuses to go any farther."
text_no:
Så han stoppet, og brødrene kom opp. Hvis noen her husker fra Shreveport, Louisiana, da jeg stod slik en dag, kom Den Hellige Ånd like før vi skulle dra og sa til meg: "Så sier Herren, vær forsiktig, det er en felle for deg i Afrika."
Jeg trodde det var hekseleger og lignende. Jeg tenkte: "Vel, jeg vet hvem Gud er, og hekseleger skal ikke plage meg." Jeg visste ikke at det gjaldt blant brødre. Ser du? Så slu var Satan.
Vi stoppet på veien. Bror Bosworth og Bror Baxter kom opp, og alle stoppet. Bror Schoeman gikk tilbake og sa: "Du må gå og snakke med ham. Han nekter å gå videre."

text:
Well, up come Brother Baxter, and got kind of huffy with me. He said, "Brother Branham, listen." He said, "We're about twelve thousand miles away from home. We're down here at the mercy of these people. And don't you think these ministers, we should consider them?"
I said, "As far as considering and being reverent to brothers, we should. But I should look to God first."
And he said, "Brother Branham, I just can't believe that it's exactly right."
Then, up comes Brother Bosworth. And Brother Bosworth's sitting here. Now watch rights right, and wrong. Brother Bosworth said, "Brother Branham, I believe you're mistaken."
And I said, "My old saintly dad that stood on the platform with me and watched the visions, and works, and wonders and so forth, and hear me say, 'Thus saith the Lord'?"
text_no:
Så kom Bror Baxter opp, og han ble litt irritert på meg. Han sa: "Bror Branham, hør her. Vi er omtrent tolv tusen mil hjemmefra. Vi er her nede på nåde fra disse menneskene. Tror du ikke vi burde ta hensyn til disse forkynnerne?"
Jeg sa: "Når det gjelder å ta hensyn og å vise ærefrykt for brødre, burde vi det. Men jeg bør først se til Gud."
Han sa: "Bror Branham, jeg kan bare ikke tro at dette er helt riktig."
Deretter kom Bror Bosworth opp. Og Bror Bosworth satt her. Nå, se hva som er rett, og hva som er galt. Bror Bosworth sa: "Bror Branham, jeg tror du tar feil."
Jeg sa: "Min gamle hellige far som sto på plattformen med meg og så visjonene, og gjerningene, og underne og så videre, og hørte meg si 'Så sier Herren'?"

text:
Brother Baxter, Brother Bosworth, lovely, both of them. Well, the ministers… Another African minister walked up and said, "Brother Branham, don't you think God speaks to somebody else besides you?"
I said, "Yes, Korah had the same idea at one time." I said, "But I don't know what."
He said, "God told us to make that itinerary."
text_no:
Bror Baxter, Bror Bosworth – begge herlige mennesker. Så kom det en annen afrikansk predikant opp og sa: "Bror Branham, tror du ikke Gud snakker til noen andre enn deg?"
Jeg svarte: "Jo, Korah hadde samme tanke en gang." Jeg la til: "Men jeg vet ikke hva."
Han fortsatte: "Gud sa til oss at vi skulle lage den reiseruten."

text:
I said, "But God told me not to take it." I said, "He might have told you, but He told me not to take it. And for my part, I'm not going to take it."
So then, there we was all hugged together on the road. All right, stopped in the middle of the road, meeting waiting. You remember it, Brother Bosworth. I said, "I'm not going to do it."
So, they went on and hum-hawed around. Said, Brother Baxter said, "Well, Brother Branham, I'd go on down to the city at least, and then pray for them people there, and we will go home." Said, "I'm just through, and tired, and sick." And Brother Baxter's very nervous, you know. And he said, "I'm just through with the whole thing. I wash my hands of it. If you can't listen to things like that," said, "I'm through with the managing of it."
And I said, "Well, you can be that too." But I said, "I'm going to listen to God, regardless." And God, if I'd only hung with that, see?
text_no:
Jeg sa: "Men Gud sa til meg at jeg ikke skulle ta det." Jeg sa: "Han kan ha sagt det til deg, men Han sa til meg at jeg ikke skulle ta det. Og for min del, kommer jeg ikke til å ta det."
Så der stod vi, klemt sammen på veien. Vi stoppet midt i veien og møtet ventet. Du husker det, Bror Bosworth. Jeg sa: "Jeg kommer ikke til å gjøre det."
De fortsatte å nøle. Bror Baxter sa: "Vel, Bror Branham, jeg ville i det minste dra ned til byen og be for folkene der, så drar vi hjem." Han sa: "Jeg er ferdig, sliten og syk." Bror Baxter er veldig nervøs, vet du. Han sa: "Jeg er ferdig med hele greia. Jeg vasker hendene mine av det. Hvis du ikke kan lytte til slike ting," sa han, "er jeg ferdig med å administrere det."
Jeg sa: "Vel, du kan være ferdig også." Men jeg sa: "Jeg kommer til å lytte til Gud, uansett." Og Gud, om jeg bare hadde holdt fast ved det, ser du?

text:
And then, I walked over there, and got some locust leaves off the tree. And pulled the locust leaves down, and put them in my hand, and there stood Brother Bosworth, Brother Baxter, and several ministers. And I walked along with these locust leaves throwing it on their feet. I said, "Thus saith the Lord, from this time on, we're out of the will of God, and it will be nothing but trouble until we return." Got back in the car crying like a baby. Set down in the car, and held my head down. Brother Schoeman glad to get me back in the car, and so away he went with me again.
We got down there in that little city, and there they was laying out there in the fields and hills, no place to go, no place to eat nothing, laying out there. That great big place back there ready for us, just moved out of it.
text_no: Deretter gikk jeg bort dit og plukket noen akasieløv fra treet. Jeg dro ned akasieløvene og holdt dem i hånden. Der sto Bror Bosworth, Bror Baxter og flere andre forkynnere. Jeg gikk langsomt og kastet akasieløvene foran føttene deres. Jeg sa: "Så sier Herren, fra og med nå er vi utenfor Guds vilje, og det vil bare bli problemer inntil vi vender tilbake." Jeg kom tilbake til bilen, gråt som et barn, satte meg ned og holdt hodet lavt. Bror Schoeman var lettet over å få meg tilbake i bilen, og så kjørte vi videre. Vi kom til den lille byen, og der lå folk ute i feltene og på åsene, uten noe sted å gå eller noe å spise. Det store stedet som tidligere var klart for oss, hadde vi nettopp forlatt.

text:
All right. Immediately, Brother Bosworth starts over, to kind of give a little beginning of the meeting. And there come up a tropical storm, and liked to drown the whole bunch. And there I was sitting down in the house, out of the will of God, just as much as Abraham was when he was in Gerar. Just sitting down there in the house. I said, "Lord, let them have a lesson of it." So, I was just sitting there in the house.
About midnight, in come Brother Bosworth, drenched. Said, "Oh, my! We tried, right."
I said, "Uh-huh."
text_no:
Rett etterpå startet Bror Bosworth møtet med en kort innledning. Så kom en tropisk storm som nesten druknet hele gruppen. Der satt jeg i huset, utenfor Guds vilje, akkurat som Abraham var da han var i Gerar. Jeg sa: "Herre, la dem lære noe av dette." Så jeg satt bare der i huset.
Omtrent ved midnatt kom Bror Bosworth inn, gjennomvåt, og sa: "Å, min! Vi forsøkte virkelig."
Jeg svarte: "Mm, riktig."

text:
So, the next day … "Oh, that will be all right," they said. "…and you've never seen a storm till you hear a tropical storm. You hear a lot to lightening flash, and roar, and after while it just [Brother Branham claps his hands together several times.] It's just like that, the lightning's flashing. Just a constant roar. Boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, the lightening a going.
And there, all of them liked to have drowned, sick people, and everything.
So then, the next morning it cleared off. Said, "Oh, this will be fine tonight." And then when it come night time, just about the time they got ready to take me, there come up a cold spell, and liked to froze them all to death." That's right.
About ten o'clock, come into the house, eleven o'clock, I said, "Uh-huh." I said, "Now, tomorrow night, we will have an earthquake." I said, "We're out of the will of God, brethren."
text_no:
Så, neste dag sa de: "Å, det vil gå bra." Og du har aldri opplevd en storm før du har hørt en tropisk storm. Du hører mye tordenbrak og lyn blinker, og etter en stund [Bror Branham klapper hendene flere ganger.] Det er akkurat slik, lynets blink. Bare et konstant brøl. Boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, boom, lynet går.
Der holdt de alle på å drukne, syke mennesker og alt.
Neste morgen klarnet det opp. De sa, "Å, det vil være fint i kveld." Men da kvelden kom, akkurat når de var klare til å ta med meg, kom det en kuldeperiode, og de holdt på å fryse i hjel. Det stemmer.
Rundt ti-tiden kom vi inn i huset, klokken elleve, og jeg sa: "Uh-huh." Jeg sa: "Nå, i morgen kveld vil vi få et jordskjelv." Jeg sa: "Vi er ute av Guds vilje, brødre."

text:
